page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core memory density. It is powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration features 16 physical cores with a single thread per core allocation, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction sets.

Local storage is provided by an integrated 800 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates divergent performance characteristics. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance reaches 3201.49, placing it in the top tier of tested instances. Conversely, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance registers at 50410.87, which falls into the average category. This variance highlights a potential tradeoff where single-threaded execution is highly competitive, but multi-threaded scaling is moderated, likely due to the shared CPU allocation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage within a shared-CPU tier, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated multi-core execution. Appropriate workloads include memory-intensive databases, caching layers, data processing tasks, and general-purpose applications requiring a large memory footprint alongside fast local storage.

Powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model with 16 physical cores and one thread per core. It includes a local 800 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark results show a top-tier single-core CPU performance score of 3201.49, contrasted by an average multi-core CPU performance score of 50410.87. This performance profile indicates strong single-threaded capabilities but moderate multi-threaded scaling under shared resource conditions. The server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, such as database hosting, caching layers, and data processing, where high memory capacity and local NVMe storage are required without the cost of dedicated CPU cores.

tagline: A memory-optimized shared instance combining high-capacity RAM and local NVMe storage with top-tier single-core processing capabilities.

Integrated 800 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation model with average multi-core scaling
Suited for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
